decile_monotonicity.interpretation: top-to-bottom decile ordering indicates whether the price/volume multisignal score carries broad monotonic information before portfolio extraction

top_bucket_readout.interpretation: compare the extreme head bucket against the next 10 names and the bottom tail to see whether TopK=10 is extracting a robust edge or a noisy slice

conclusion: The price/volume multisignal score does not show convincing positive full-sample IC, so the family likely lacks usable gross edge in its current fixed form.
Ranks 11-20 outperform the top 10 on average, which suggests the current TopK=10 extraction is cutting too aggressively or too noisily at the head.
Decile ordering is weak or inverted, which suggests the family may not carry stable cross-sectional ordering information.
Use this diagnosis before deciding whether to continue the price/volume multisignal family or switch to a different score family.
